entry.title: Joule is Not Magic - Here is the Architecture Behind It
entry.content:
SAP Community already has a great beginner series on Joule. This post is for architects and consultants who already know what Joule is and want to understand how it actually works under the hood.

Before going further, a quick scope note. This post focuses on Joule as an end-user AI copilot embedded within SAP business applications such as S/4HANA, SuccessFactors and Ariba. SAP has since expanded the Joule portfolio to include Joule Studio, Joule for Developers and Joule for Consultants, among others. The grounding architecture described here is most directly relevant to Joule's role as a business copilot, though many of the underlying mechanisms apply across the portfolio.

What is Grounding and Why Does it Matter?

Without grounding, an AI answers questions based on its general training data. The responses may sound fluent and confident but have nothing to do with your organisation's actual data, processes or documents.

Grounding connects the AI to your specific business context. It combines generative LLMs with information retrieval techniques to improve response quality and accuracy, without the complexity and cost of fine-tuning a model on company-specific data. The result is responses that are relevant, accurate and actionable.

This is not a nice to have. It is the difference between a generic chatbot and an enterprise grade AI copilot.

How Joule is Grounded

Joule does not rely on a single grounding mechanism. It combines multiple approaches, each suited to a different type of data and scenario. 

SAP Help Documentation

Joule retrieves information from SAP's official Help Portal and documentation using Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). SAP's documentation corpus covers millions of pages across all SAP applications. It is processed using chunking, vectorisation and embedding techniques, then stored in the SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine for semantic retrieval.

Ask Joule a procedural question like "How do I create a purchase order in Ariba?" and it retrieves the most relevant documentation chunks and generates a contextual answer grounded in SAP's official content. This layer is managed entirely by SAP. Customers do not configure it.

SAP Domain Models

SAP Domain Models are AI models trained specifically on SAP domain knowledge - including code, data, metadata, business processes, architecture knowledge and documentation. When a user initiates an enquiry or wishes to create code, these models are designed to provide results firmly grounded in the SAP context rather than relying on generic internet knowledge.

Combined with context graphs and agents, SAP Domain Models bring deep SAP knowledge directly to Joule, Joule Agents and SAP applications. This is a distinct grounding layer that complements documentation retrieval - while SAP Help Documentation retrieves relevant content at query time, SAP Domain Models carry SAP knowledge baked into the model itself.
https://www.sap.com/products/artificial-intelligence/ai-models/domain-models.html

Document Grounding

For organisations that need Joule to answer questions based on internal knowledge, SAP AI Core provides document grounding capabilities. Think SOPs, process guides, compliance policies, onboarding manuals.

The process works as follows. Documents are retrieved from connected enterprise content repositories, chunked and embedded into vector representations, then stored in the SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine. When a user asks a question, a similarity search retrieves the most relevant chunks, which are then injected into Joule's context to generate a grounded response.

Out of the box, Microsoft SharePoint is the primary supported connector for Joule's built-in shared document grounding service. Supported file formats include PDF, Word, HTML and common image formats. Content is refreshed daily.

Organisations that need broader connectivity can use SAP AI Core's private document grounding pattern, which supports additional repositories including SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Document Management Service, Google Drive, AWS S3, SFTP and ServiceNow.
https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-ai-core/generative-ai/grounding-035c455a5a424697b60f4a24b6d791fe

SAP Business Data Cloud and SAP Knowledge Graph

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) provides the unified data foundation - federating and harmonising data from S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, Ariba and other SAP and non-SAP applications into managed data products. These data products are made available directly on the SAP Generative AI Hub, which grounds the LLM in trusted organisational data. This is the architectural link between BDC and Joule: BDC data products flow into the Generative AI Hub, which uses them to inform Joule's responses.

But data alone is not enough. Joule also needs to understand the meaning and relationships between business objects across systems. An Employee in SuccessFactors and a Cost Center Owner in S/4HANA are the same person. A Vendor in Ariba and a Supplier in S/4HANA Finance refer to the same entity.

This is where SAP Knowledge Graph comes in. Working alongside BDC, it provides fully managed ontologies that help Joule understand processes, relationships and context across the enterprise. Because Joule uses SAP BDC data products and the SAP Knowledge Graph, it can use fully managed ontologies to understand processes, relationships and context across the enterprise, enabling more comprehensive and accurate insight.

BDC and Knowledge Graph are distinct but work together. BDC provides the data. Knowledge Graph provides the semantic understanding of what that data means and how it relates.

Underpinning all of this is SAP HANA Cloud, the database at the core of BDC. It brings together vectors, graphs, relational and spatial data with business context in a single in-memory database, helping to ensure reliable agent reasoning.

Direct API and OData Integration

For scenarios requiring live transactional data, Joule can invoke SAP application APIs directly rather than relying on replicated analytical data.

What is worth highlighting here is that Joule does not just read data. It can act on it. For example, Creating a purchase order, approving a workflow item, triggering an automation via SAP Build Process Automation - all through natural language. This is where Joule moves from being an informational copilot to an operational AI agent.

How it All Fits Together

The foundation for everything described above is the SAP Business AI Platform - the infrastructure layer that brings together SAP AI Core, the Generative AI Hub, BDC data products and the grounding pipeline. Before any grounding mechanism is invoked, Joule performs intent detection and orchestration within this platform, routing queries to the appropriate grounding source. 

User Question
      ↓
Intent Detection and Orchestration
(SAP Generative AI Hub on SAP Business AI Platform)
      ↓
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ SAP Help Documentation (HANA Cloud Vector Engine)              │
│ SAP Domain Models (SAP-trained, context graph grounded)        │
│ Document Grounding (SharePoint / AI Core connectors)           │
│ SAP Business Data Cloud data products                          │
│ SAP Knowledge Graph (ontology and semantic layer)              │
│ SAP HANA Cloud (vectors, graphs, relational, spatial)          │
│ Direct API → SAP Applications (read and write)                 │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
      ↓
LLM reasoning over grounded context
      ↓
Response or Action

What This Means for Your AI Roadmap

Organisations planning a Joule rollout should assess their grounding readiness before going live, not after.

SAP Help documentation grounding and SAP Domain Models require nothing from the customer - SAP manages them. For the remaining layers, there are real questions to answer. Where does your internal knowledge live, and is SharePoint available or do you need a private grounding setup? Is SAP Business Data Cloud part of your landscape - if not, the cross-system grounding will be limited. Are you tracking the maturity of SAP Knowledge Graph for your specific applications? And for agentic scenarios, what authorisation and governance controls need to be in place before Joule starts executing business actions?

These are not technical edge cases. They are architecture decisions that directly determine how useful Joule will be in practice.

entry.title: Innovation Workshop ‘Accelerate Consulting Productivity with SAP Joule 4 Consultants' June 17, 2026
entry.content:
Dear Valued Customer,

The Innovation Workshop focusing on ‘Accelerate Consulting Productivity with SAP Joule for Consultants (J4C)’ took place virtually on June 17, 2026. Please find the agenda for the workshop here.

Please find the recording of the workshop here, the slides shown during the sessions here and the Q&A section here.

The Innovation workshop provided an overview of SAP Joule for Consultants (J4C), highlighting its architecture, UI & Roadmap.

 

 It furthermore provided a live activation of J4C following the main steps from the Access Setup and the validation of the prerequisites to the Configuration Steps and Readiness Validation. The end-to-end setup took appr 1h.

After the setup was completed, the Functional Onboarding steps were outlined. In addition, typical Use Cases and Prompting strategies were demonstrated.

This workshop concluded with key takeaways and further explanation on the commercial model, the availability, the key value proposition and its roadmap and future features. More than 30 customer questions were answered by SAP Product Experts and consultants.

 

Thank you for attending and we hope you benefited from this workshop.

entry.title: From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills This June
entry.content:
In the age of AI, the skills we build today will define how we work tomorrow. That’s why we’ve launched a monthly blog series that spotlights the latest AI learning assets and upcoming learning opportunities. 

Building on my first blog, where I introduced you to our AI live sessions Beyond Hype (which are all about getting productive with SAP Business AI), I wanted to address some of your most asked questions.  

  1. How are agents audited and monitored, and is there a mechanism to review and approve or deny actions before execution, or do they operate with full autonomy?
    SAP handles the security of AI agentic workflows from the design phase onwards in the following ways: Guidelines and controls applied to the hosting environment, proper authorization checks, keeping humans in loop for critical decisions or actions, and by limiting actions to a predefined list. Only after human approval does the agent execute the transaction, respecting role-based access controls.

  2. Which large language model powers Joule?      
    SAP maintains partnerships with leading large language model providers and selects the best‑performing model for each specific Joule or agent use case based on rigorous benchmark testing. As a result, the underlying model may vary by scenario to ensure optimal quality, performance, and fit.

  3. Can you give an overview of the lines of businesses that are available in the Joule Early Adopter Care (EAC) Program?
    If you followed the announcements at Sapphire 2026, you've probably noticed that Joule is evolving rapidly, offering a new generation of experiences with Joule Work, Joule Desktop, and Joule Studio 2.0. Therefore, SAP will launch Early Adopter Care (EAC) programs that give selected customers early access to upcoming Joule innovations while working directly with SAP product teams. To answer your question: SAP S4/HANA Public and Private Cloud Edition, SAP SuccessFactors, and SAP Ariba will be part of the Joule EAC programs.

Want to dive deeper into the content? Rewatch the session and access the session material: 

  • All Things Business AI: Latest Insights, Updates & Live Demos (recording)  

Everything we covered in this blog so far, from agents, human-in-the-loop design, to early adopter programs, is part of a much larger shift in how SAP is building for the future. For the full picture of what's coming, the Innovation News Guide from SAP Sapphire 2026 lays it out clearly:

  • Joule Work redefines how users engage with SAP software. Instead of navigating individual applications and entering data across several screens, users will interact primarily with Joule by describing a desired business outcome and letting Joule orchestrate the right combination of workflows, data, and agents to get it done. Sound familiar? That's the same human-in-the-loop approach we walked through earlier in this blog. See it in action in the Interactive Value Journey.
  • The SAP Business AI Platform is a new foundation for building and deploying enterprise AI grounded in business context. SAP Business AI Platform now unifies SAP Business Technology Platform, SAP Business Data Cloud, and SAP Business AI into a single, governed environment. Explore how the agents and applications you build understand your business, not just your prompts, in this Interactive Value Journey.

The shifts we've just explored are moving fast and the best way to keep pace is to learn alongside the people building them. Sign up for the SAP AI Adoption and Learning Week, July 6-9: a week of live, expert-led sessions combining strategic insights with practical guidance. The virtual event is free of charge, no SAP Learning Hub subscription required, just an S-user ID to register for individual sessions.

Stay tuned for more, and happy learning! 🚀 

Further Resources: 

  • From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills This May
  • From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills This March  
  • From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills This February 
  • From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills This January 
  • From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills This December 
  • From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills This November 
  • From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills This October

Joule Studio, classic edition Joule #SAP Business AI Platform

entry.title: Creation of Joule skill using API
entry.content:
1. Add destination(mostly via basis team)

2.Add Action 

Choose an API Source ( Depending on API )

 

In my case my API was added into my System using /n/iwfnd/maint_serivce , So I have selected Odata  Destination , if it's RAP API use the second one.

Now select the destination(Destination is just the system you have integrated)

Enter the resource path ( from /sap/opu****  in case of maint_service or RAP )

then next and select/add all the entity which you wanted to use as action and PUBLISH/RELEASE 

You can test the action there as well.

Now let's go to Create joule skill.

Go to lobby and Click on create button select joule agent and skill , Provide any contextual name 

This type of Interface will be visible to you , Click on create again select joule skill , Give any name along with description 

This type of interface will be displayed to you , now click on ADD(+) Button

Then select Call Action and again click on Browse all Action 

search your action add it.  If your action is dependent on parameters such(Let's say your api provides response based on certain values ypu passed

Tap here , In Parameters you can add your input skills.

Now Tap on your action and go to input parameters 

Map it accordingly all the mandatory fields 

Only this will be enough to call API

But as we wanted to display our result in chatbot itself 

Go To END and add output skills , Map it accordingly again but with result ( Because we need to display result )

Now save and Test (Destination System),

You will be able to see result( Let me share you some sample result )

You can see , Chat results only values select and mapped in output skills where as when you tapped on api call at right you will be able to see complete result.

 

FAQ- Prabhatm580@gmail.com

entry.title: Creating an SAP SD Billing Document Using a BAPI in SAP Build and Accessing It Through 'Joule Work'
entry.content:
Hi Everyone!

Recently, I was working on a requirement to create an SD billing document in SAP S/4HANA using SAP Build Process Automation and make it accessible through the Joule Work mobile app.

Due to some internal requirements & technical restrictions, the only option available to me was to use the BAPI_BILLINGDOC_CREATEMULTIPLE BAPI to create the billing document.

I am sharing the following demo which I created in the test environment. To use this BAPI in SAP Build, there are two main configurations required:

1. Map the virtual host to the internal host in SAP Cloud Connector using the RFC protocol, and expose the required BAPI or RFC function module.

2. Create a destination of type RFC in the SAP BTP Cockpit.

Let's first see how Cloud to On-Premises mapping in Cloud Connector looks like:

We also need to enable the required resources, such as the BAPIs and function modules that we want to consume in SAP Build. The RFC_FUNCTION_SEARCH function module enables the search for function modules from SAP Build.



And this is how the destination in BTP looks:



The virtual message server is configured in the Cloud Connector and mapped to the internal SAP S/4HANA host, and logon group information is available in SMLG.



Logon group can be found here :



Now that the Cloud Connector and BTP destination configurations are complete, let’s log in to SAP Build and see how we can create an action using BAPI_BILLINGDOC_CREATEMULTIPLE.

Select RFC's, BAPI's:



All the BAPIs & RFCs from backend will be available to use. We will go ahead and look for BAPI/RFC we'd like to use, and create a new project.



The action is now available in Connectors -> Actions, and can be used in our build projects.



Using this action, I created a custom process that can be triggered to generate a billing document by entering the reference sales document number.



Let's see it in action;
Assume we have below sales order, for which we'd like to generate a billing document.



Let’s now access the custom process we created. I have added the form URL to SAP Build Work Zone for easy access.





Since I added an email notification step to the process, we receive an email once the billing document is generated successfully.



Let's validated the generated billing document number in the system.



To access SAP Build Work Zone applications and tiles in the Joule mobile app, open Settings, scan the QR code, and complete the authentication process.





As we can see below, all our SAP Build Work Zone applications are now accessible through the Joule Work mobile app as well.





Thanks for reading!

Stay tuned for more demos on AI agents using Joule Studio, n8n, and automation in SAP Build.

entry.title: Joule Work mobile app Version 3.1 is out
entry.content:
With the latest release of Joule Work mobile app Version 3.1, the app provides exciting features for both, the New Experience (Engagement Layer), as well as the SAP Build Work Zone Experience – formally called SAP Mobile Start.

Let me start with the features that are valid for both experiences.
Based on customer feedback, the app allows you now to customise the Support Section on the Settings screen. This allows you to add additional information for your users, in case they have any support needs. Administrators can easily add information, such as phone numbers, email addresses, and websites for further end user support. Once configured, the info is displayed in the Profile and Settings screen as well as during the onboarding process. The feature can be added and configured via SAP Mobile Services or via Mobile Device Management (MDM). Please also check our SAP Help page for details.

We also have updated the supported languages for SAP Joule Work and added: Greek, Croatian, Indonesian, Romanian, Ukrainian, and Vietnamese.

Now, let us have a look at the features that are relevant for the SAP Build Work Zone Experience.
First and most important, you can now directly access workspaces from SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition - via a dedicated screen that is available in the navigation bar. This ensures that you get direct access to your most needed content from anywhere in the app. In case you work with many workspaces, you can easily filter them, for instance if you only want to see your favourites – or search for them. As soon as this feature got enabled by the admin,  the SAP Build Work Zone Workspaces screen will be visible.  

Screenshot: SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition – Workspaces

Second, users can now change the language of external SAP Build Work Zone tiles, like news and to-dos directly inside the app, from the settings screen.

Now, let us have a look at the new features for our Joule Work New Experience.
As introduced in my last blog “Joule Work, mobile app is here”, Spaces are one of the key elements of the New Experience. Lets recap: Spaces are dynamic, intent-driven workspaces for more complex business problems. They are composed in real time to bring the right data from different systems, tools, and context together for a specific goal, freeing people from working in multiple applications and passive UIs. With the new release, you can now pin your favourite spaces to the top of the spaces list, what will make it much easier for you to access your favourite content. To do so, just do a long tap on the space that you want to pin and the menu appears. Here, you can easily rename your space, delete it or pin/ unpin it. Joule Work New Experience is currently available within an Early Adapter Care program.

 Screenshot: Pin favourite Spaces to the top of the list

I hope you will enjoy these features in the Joule Work mobile app.

Visit SAP Mobile Experience Community Page and click “follow” to get the latest development and innovation of our solutions. We look forward to hearing about your experience with setting up the solution in your landscape; please do share your thoughts and comments below. Enter here for additional questions regarding SAP Mobile Experience Applications.

Want to be notified? Check your profile settings to ensure you have your settings activated.

entry.title: Bridging the Gap: Connecting SAP Digital Manufacturing to Joule via SAP Build Studio
entry.content:
Enabling Joule for SAP Digital Manufacturing with an MCP Server

This article explains how to extend Joule with live SAP Digital Manufacturing context by exposing SAP DM APIs through a Model Context Protocol server deployed on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry.

Why Joule Needs SAP DM Connectivity

Joule is positioned as the AI copilot across SAP’s cloud suite, but it does not automatically understand what is happening on the shop floor unless SAP Digital Manufacturing data is made available to it. For example, if a user asks about today’s OEE, open non-conformances, delayed production orders, or work center performance, Joule needs access to live execution-layer data before it can answer accurately.

Without SAP DM connectivity, Joule lacks access to real-time production order status, OEE and performance data, quality inspection and non-conformance records, shift and crew context, material consumption, scrap, process parameters, and operator annotations.

In short, Joule can provide planning-layer intelligence, but it cannot reliably answer execution-layer questions without SAP DM connectivity. The implementation below bridges that gap by using an MCP server as the integration layer between Joule and SAP Digital Manufacturing.

Solution Overview

The solution uses an MCP server to wrap selected SAP Digital Manufacturing APIs and expose them as callable tools for Joule. Joule sends a user request to the MCP server, the MCP server calls SAP DM using service-bound credentials, and the response is returned to Joule with live shop floor context.

 

The high-level flow is: user question in Joule, tool selection by the agent, MCP tool execution, SAP DM API call, live SAP DM response, and final conversational answer from Joule.

What MCP Does in This Architecture

Model Context Protocol is an open protocol that allows AI assistants to discover and call external tools and data sources in a standardized way. Instead of building a custom integration for every AI client, the MCP server exposes business capabilities once and makes them available to any compatible AI client.

In this setup, the MCP server hosted on BTP CF on DM tenant, acts as a facade over SAP Digital Manufacturing APIs. It converts SAP DM capabilities into reusable tools that Joule can call during a conversation, such as retrieving SFCs for an order, checking quality records, or reading execution status.

Project Structure

sap_dm_mcp/

├── server.js          ← MCP server, tool definitions, Express endpoint

├── dm_client.js       ← SAP DM API client with token caching

├── btp_config.js      ← Config loader (VCAP_SERVICES + env vars)

├── .env.example       ← Local dev environment variable template

├── manifest.yml       ← BTP Cloud Foundry deployment config

├── xsuaa-config.json  ← XSUAA service OAuth configuration

└── Procfile           ← CF startup command

Configure SAP DM Service Binding and Credential Loading

1. Create the XSUAA Service

The MCP server first needs an XSUAA service instance so the Cloud Foundry application can support OAuth-based authentication.

cf create-service xsuaa application sap-dm-mcp-xsuaa -c xsuaa-config.json

This service is used by the MCP application runtime on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry and should be bound to the MCP server application.

2. Bind the SAP Digital Manufacturing Service

For SAP DM access, the MCP server reads the Digital Manufacturing service binding directly from Cloud Foundry instead of relying on a manually configured destination for the SAP DM API call.

In the SAP BTP cockpit, open the subaccount where SAP Digital Manufacturing is provisioned, go to the Digital Manufacturing service instance, create a binding for the MCP server application, and ensure that the binding exposes the SAP DM API endpoint and OAuth credentials.

3. Load Credentials from VCAP_SERVICES

When the application is deployed to Cloud Foundry, bound service credentials are injected into VCAP_SERVICES. The btp_config.js file reads the Digital Manufacturing service binding and extracts the SAP DM API base URL and OAuth token details. It is important to bind digital-manufacturing-service to MCP server app so that MCP is able to retrieve and utilize service credentials for api call. The destination services on xsuaa can allow only reading app destinations but here we need something similar to BTP destination.

//Load using
const vcap = process.env.VCAP_SERVICES;

//Read the service creds

const dmService = vcap["digital-manufacturing-services-quality"]?.[0];

config.dmBaseUrl = dmService?.credentials?.["public-api-endpoint"];

config.destination = {

  clientId: dmService?.credentials?.uaa?.clientid,

  clientSecret: dmService?.credentials?.uaa?.clientsecret,

  tokenUrl: dmService?.credentials?.uaa?.url + "/oauth/token"

};

This approach avoids hardcoding credentials and keeps the application portable across environments, provided the correct SAP DM service binding exists.

Implement Token Handling in dm_client.js

// Token cache

let _destToken, _destExpiresAt, _destBaseUrl;

async function fetchDestinationToken(config) {

  if (_destToken && Date.now() < _destExpiresAt - 30_000) {

    return { token: _destToken, baseUrl: _destBaseUrl };

  }

// Fetch fresh token from DM service binding's token URL

  const resp = await fetch(config.destination.tokenUrl, {

    method: "POST",

    headers: {

      Authorization: "Basic " + Buffer.from(

        `${config.destination.clientId}:${config.destination.clientSecret}`

      ).toString("base64"),

      "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded"

    },

    body: "grant_type=client_credentials"

  });

const data = await resp.json();

  _destToken = data.access_token;

  _destExpiresAt = Date.now() + data.expires_in * 1000;

  _destBaseUrl = config.dmBaseUrl;

  return { token: _destToken, baseUrl: _destBaseUrl };

}

The client caches the OAuth token and refreshes it only when required, using a 30-second buffer before expiry. This avoids unnecessary token requests while keeping API calls authenticated.

HTTP Basic Authentication is used for the token request, which handles special characters in the client secret cleanly compared with passing credentials through form encoding.

export async function dmGet(endpoint, params = {}) {

  const { token, baseUrl } = await resolveAuth(await loadConfig());

  const url = buildUrl(baseUrl, endpoint, params);

  const resp = await fetch(url, { headers: authHeaders(token) });

  return handleResponse(resp, endpoint);

}

Build the MCP Server and Register SAP DM Tools

The server.js file is the main application entry point. It creates an Express application, exposes a POST /mcp endpoint using Streamable HTTP transport, and registers SAP Digital Manufacturing tools that Joule can discover and call.

import express from "express";

import { McpServer } from "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/mcp.js";

import { StreamableHTTPServerTransport } from "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/streamableHttp.js";

const app = express();

app.use(express.json());

app.post("/mcp", async (req, res) => {

  const transport = new StreamableHTTPServerTransport({ sessionIdGenerator: undefined });

  const server = createServer();

  await server.connect(transport);

  await transport.handleRequest(req, res, req.body);

});

app.get("/health", (_, res) => res.json({ status: "ok" }));

app.listen(process.env.PORT || 3000);

Deploy the MCP Server to SAP BTP Cloud Foundry

The manifest.yml file defines the Cloud Foundry application name, runtime, memory, health check endpoint, service bindings, and environment variables required for deployment.

applications:

  - name: sap-dm-mcp-server

    memory: 256M

    disk_quota: 512M

    instances: 1

    buildpacks:

      - nodejs_buildpack

    command: node server.js

    health-check-type: http

    health-check-http-endpoint: /health

    services:

      - sap-dm-mcp-xsuaa

      - sap-dm-destination

    env:

      DM_BASE_URL: https://api.test.region.dmc.cloud.sap

      DM_PLANT: PLANT1

      DM_USE_DESTINATION: "true"

      DM_DESTINATION_NAME: "EXE"

      NODE_ENV: production

After deployment, the bound services populate VCAP_SERVICES automatically. The application then reads the required SAP DM endpoint and OAuth credentials without manual credential entry.

Validate the Deployment

After pushing the application to Cloud Foundry, test the health endpoint using the generated application route with the /health suffix.

GET https://sap-dm-mcp-server.<your-cf-domain>/health

A successful deployment returns a response such as { "status": "ok" }. Once this is working, the MCP server is ready to be connected to Joule through SAP Build Studio.

Connect the MCP Server to Joule in SAP Build Studio

Create a new agent project in SAP Build Studio and add a Joule agent. Define the agent’s expertise, instructions, and supporting context prompts. During development, use a lower reasoning or thinking setting to control token usage, then adjust the model configuration before publishing for wider use.

Create a destination for the MCP server in the Joule subaccount, using no authentication. Add this destination in Studio Control Tower so Joule can communicate with the MCP server.

The destination is used by Joule to reach the MCP server. The MCP server itself uses the SAP DM service binding to authenticate against SAP Digital Manufacturing, so the Joule destination is not used for SAP DM authentication.

Release and Test the Joule Agent

To release the project, ensure that a shared environment exists. If you do not have permissions to create it, request support from an administrator before publishing the agent.

After release, go to Control Tower, open the assistant, and confirm that the new agent is available. Test it with a SAP DM-specific question, such as asking for the list of SFCs for a production order. If the configuration is correct, Joule should call the MCP tool, retrieve SAP DM data, and return a contextual response. Below are some tests I ran:
Prompt: Use my agent and get the data for production orders for Material AIRP_PURIFIER_FG and plant 4101. Analyse it considering the orders released in last 3 months and based on it forecast the orders which can be planned for next 6 months.

 

 

 

 

You can see that Joule now can retrieve production insights from execution and provide analysis/predictions. Hope you find value in this blog.

entry.title: How We Built an Audit and Compliance Solution for SAP SuccessFactors HCM Using SAP BTP and Joule
entry.content:
Introduction
In most organizations using SAP SuccessFactors HCM, configuration changes and employee data updates happen every day. New fields are added, business rules are modified, permissions are updated, and employee records are continuously maintained across multiple system environments.

During our work with SAP SuccessFactors implementations, we frequently observed how difficult it was for administrators and compliance teams to maintain visibility into these changes. Organizations often operate multiple environments such as Development, Test, and Production, and even small configuration differences between them can lead to unexpected issues, failed deployments, or inconsistent business processes.

At the same time, tracking employee data changes is not always straightforward. Understanding who modified a record, when the change occurred, what values were updated, or whether a record was deleted often requires navigating multiple modules and reports. Preparing for audits or compliance reviews can quickly become a time-consuming and manual process.

We also noticed that maintaining configuration documentation was another major challenge. As systems evolve, documentation often becomes outdated, making it difficult for teams to validate configurations, troubleshoot issues, or ensure compliance with organizational standards.

The real challenge was not simply collecting audit information—it was providing a centralized and intelligent way to compare configurations, track changes, monitor compliance activities, and generate accurate documentation across the entire SAP SuccessFactors landscape.

This realization led us to build an Audit and Compliance Solution for SAP SuccessFactors HCM using SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) and SAP Joule. The solution was designed to help organizations improve governance, strengthen compliance, reduce manual effort, and gain complete visibility into both configuration and employee data changes.

Our Approach
To address these challenges, we built an Audit and Compliance Solution for SAP SuccessFactors HCM on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) and integrated it with SAP Joule to provide a centralized and intelligent auditing experience.

While designing this solution, one of our primary goals was to simplify how organizations monitor, validate, and govern their SAP SuccessFactors landscape. Instead of relying on multiple reports, manual comparisons, and fragmented audit information, we wanted to bring everything together into a single platform that provides complete visibility into system and employee-related changes.

What mattered most during development was usability and transparency. Administrators should be able to quickly identify configuration differences between environments, track employee data modifications, review permission changes, monitor recruitment activities, and generate documentation without spending hours collecting information from different sources.

We also wanted to reduce the dependency on manual audits and documentation efforts. By automating metadata comparison, workbook generation, and change tracking, the solution enables organizations to focus on analysis and decision-making rather than data collection.

With this approach, organizations can confidently maintain consistency across environments, strengthen compliance processes, improve governance, and gain complete visibility into configuration and employee data changes while significantly reducing operational effort.

Enhancing the Experience with SAP Joule

While the Audit and Compliance Solution provides comprehensive auditing capabilities, integrating it with Joule makes accessing audit information faster and more intuitive.

Instead of navigating multiple screens or manually searching through audit reports, administrators can interact with the solution using natural language. Joule interprets user requests and helps retrieve relevant audit information, compare configurations, and initiate documentation generation through a conversational interface.

Some examples include:

  • Compare metadata between Development, Test, and Production environments.
  • Retrieve employee data audit history.
  • Review Role-Based Permission (RBP) changes.
  • View deleted employee records.
  • Generate configuration or business rule workbooks.
  • Access audit insights using natural language prompts.

This conversational experience reduces manual navigation, improves productivity, and enables administrators to quickly access the information required for governance, compliance, and troubleshooting

 


Key Features and Benefits

1. Metadata Comparison Across Systems

  • One of the biggest challenges organizations face is ensuring configuration consistency across Development, Test, and Production environments. Even small differences in metadata configurations can lead to unexpected behavior, deployment issues, or business process disruptions.
  • To address this, the solution provides a comprehensive metadata comparison capability that analyzes configurations across multiple SAP SuccessFactors environments. Users can quickly identify differences at both entity and field levels, including properties such as field type, required status, visibility settings, and other configuration attributes.
  • What makes this capability valuable is the ability to drill down into detailed comparison results while also providing a summarized view for faster analysis. This significantly reduces the effort required to validate configurations and troubleshoot deployment issues.

 

2. Configuration Workbook Generation

  • Maintaining configuration documentation is often a manual and time-consuming task. As SuccessFactors environments evolve, documentation can quickly become outdated, making audits and validations more difficult.
  • The solution automatically generates configuration workbooks directly from SuccessFactors metadata. Users can select specific modules, entities, and fields to create structured Excel-based documentation.
  • This ensures organizations always have access to accurate and up-to-date configuration baselines while significantly reducing manual documentation effort.

 

3. Employee Data Change Audit

  • Tracking employee data changes is a critical requirement for both compliance and operational governance. However, identifying who changed a record, what was changed, and when the change occurred often requires significant manual investigation.
  • The Employee Data Audit capability provides complete visibility into employee-related modifications by capturing change history, event details, modification timestamps, and responsible users.
  • This enables HR and compliance teams to quickly investigate changes, improve accountability, and maintain audit readiness.

 


4. Timesheet Audit

  • Timesheet data plays an important role in workforce management and payroll processes. Organizations often require visibility into timesheet updates, approvals, and historical modifications.
  • The Timesheet Audit feature provides detailed tracking of employee timesheet activities, including modification history, status updates, and audit records.
  • This helps organizations improve transparency while supporting compliance and reporting requirements.

 


5. Benefits Audit

  • Employee benefits data frequently changes throughout the employee lifecycle. Maintaining visibility into these changes is essential for compliance and operational accuracy.
  • The Benefits Audit module enables organizations to track benefit-related updates, view modification history, identify responsible users, and export audit information for reporting purposes.
  • This provides greater transparency and simplifies compliance reviews.


6. Role-Based Permission (RBP) Audit

  • Managing user permissions is one of the most critical aspects of governance and security within SAP SuccessFactors.
  • The RBP Audit capability allows organizations to monitor permission changes, review modified roles, identify responsible administrators, and track historical updates.
  • By providing complete visibility into permission-related activities, organizations can strengthen security controls and support compliance initiatives.


7. Recruitment Auditing

  • Recruitment processes involve continuous activity across job requisitions and candidate applications. Monitoring these activities is important for operational oversight and compliance reporting.
  • The solution provides dedicated auditing capabilities for both Job Applications and Job Requisitions, enabling users to track recruitment activity, review status changes, monitor modification history, and generate reports when needed.
  • This helps organizations maintain transparency throughout the recruitment lifecycle.

 


8. Deleted Records Tracking

  • Deleted employee records can create significant challenges during audits and investigations if proper tracking mechanisms are not available.
  • The solution identifies deleted records and provides details about when the deletion occurred and who performed the action. This helps organizations maintain historical visibility and supports compliance investigations when required.


9. Historical Audit Record Management

  • As audit data grows over time, organizations need efficient ways to manage historical information while complying with retention policies.
  • The solution provides capabilities to review and purge historical audit records in a controlled manner. This helps manage data volume, improve system performance, and maintain compliance with organizational retention requirements.

 

 

10. Business Rule Extraction and Workbook Generation

  • Business rules are a critical component of SuccessFactors implementations, yet documenting them manually can be challenging and error-prone.
  • The solution automatically extracts business rules from the system and generates structured workbooks for documentation and review purposes.
  • This improves documentation accuracy, simplifies governance processes, and reduces ongoing maintenance effort.

Business Value

  • This solution delivers significant value by helping organizations strengthen governance, improve compliance, and reduce manual effort associated with auditing and documentation activities.
  • One of the biggest advantages is improved visibility. Administrators and compliance teams can quickly identify configuration differences across environments, monitor employee data changes, and track critical system activities from a centralized platform.
  • The solution also improves audit readiness by providing detailed tracking of employee updates, permission changes, recruitment activities, and deleted records. Instead of spending hours collecting information from multiple sources, teams can access the required audit information in a single place.
  • Another important benefit is the reduction of manual effort. Tasks such as metadata comparison, workbook generation, business rule extraction, and audit reporting are automated, allowing teams to focus on analysis and decision-making rather than data collection.
  • By providing accurate documentation and complete change visibility, the solution helps organizations improve data quality, maintain configuration consistency, and reduce operational risks across their SAP SuccessFactors landscape.
  • Finally, because the solution is built on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP), it offers a secure, scalable, and enterprise-ready foundation that can support organizations of all sizes.

Conclusion
Managing configurations, employee data changes, permissions, and compliance activities across SAP SuccessFactors environments can quickly become a complex and time-consuming task. As organizations grow, maintaining visibility into these activities becomes increasingly important for governance, operational efficiency, and regulatory compliance.

The Audit and Compliance Solution for SAP SuccessFactors HCM addresses these challenges by providing a centralized platform for metadata comparison, employee data auditing, recruitment tracking, permission monitoring, and automated documentation generation.

From our experience working with SAP SuccessFactors implementations, we have seen how difficult it can be to manually track changes, validate configurations, and prepare for audits. By automating these processes and bringing critical audit information together in a single platform, organizations can significantly reduce effort while improving transparency and control.

Ultimately, this solution is not just about auditing system activities—it is about enabling organizations to operate with greater confidence. By providing complete visibility into changes across the SAP SuccessFactors landscape, organizations can strengthen compliance, improve governance, and focus on driving business value rather than managing manual audit processes.

entry.title: Joule Work & Knowledge Graphs - From Thought to Execution in Seconds - No Clicks. Just Results.
entry.content:
What if enterprise systems could execute what you have in mind—your goal, your need—without requiring you to navigate applications, workflows, or data manually?

That’s exactly what we’ll explore in this special live session on July 8 (15:00 CEST / 09:00 New York / 18:30 Bengaluru).

 Why This Matters

Today’s enterprises are full of complexity—fragmented systems, disconnected data, and manual steps.

This session shows how to:

  • Move from describing a need to seeing it executed
  • Let AI orchestrate processes across systems automatically
  • Use the Knowledge Graph to ensure every action is grounded in real business context
  • Deliver systems that feel simpler, faster, and more aligned with how people think

Who Should Join

This is relevant for anyone shaping or using enterprise systems:

  • Developers
  • Architects
  • Data & AI professionals
  • Product managers
  • Business analysts
  • Transformation & innovation leaders
  • IT decision-makers

What You’ll Learn

  • How Joule Work translates goals into actions
  • How Knowledge Graphs bring context and meaning to AI
  • What needs to be designed:
    • APIs
    • Event-driven architectures
    • Semantic models

Join Us Live

📅July 8
⏰15:00 CEST / 09:00 EDT/ 18:30 IST

💬Why join live?
Interact directly with the expert, ask questions, and get instant insights.

⏯️ Can’t make it?
Watch the video anytime using the same YouTube link.

https://events.sap.com/data-analytics/en_us/home.html

https://youtube.com/live/mr0U48Ay_ys
